title: Telegram webhook requires HTTPS configuration
symptom: When trying to use a Telegram node to trigger a workflow, an error occurs indicating that the webhook cannot be registered due to HTTP usage.
not_this_if: Webhook is configured with a public HTTPS URL
root_cause: Telegram requires webhooks to be registered with a publicly accessible HTTPS URL. If n8n is configured to use HTTP or a localhost address, Telegram cannot send events to it, resulting in an error.
fix_steps: Set the WEBHOOK_URL parameter to a publicly accessible HTTPS URL.
If you don't have a domain, consider using ngrok or a similar service to expose your local server.
Restart your n8n instance to apply the changes.
agent_instruction: Configure the WEBHOOK_URL in your n8n settings to use a public HTTPS URL.
